person.name: Joan Grelley
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Daughter of Robert Grelley, 7th Baron of Manchester & Hawise de Burgh
1333 - heir of brother Thomas Grelley, 7th Baron of Manchester
1309 - her brother Thomas Grelley gave & granted to Sir John de la Warre & Joan his wife, [Thomas's sister] & the heirs of their bodies, the manor of Manchester & Advowson 
Nov 1307 - enrollment of the debt of Thomas, Lord Grelley to John de la Warre & Joan his wife, sister of said Thomas in the huge amount of £6,000 sterling was levied in default on his lands in Lancaster, Lincoln, Northampton, Rutland, Norfolk, & Somerset
23 Oct 1310 - John de la Warre received royal pardon for acquiring from his brother-in-law, the manor of Wakely, without License & License was granted to said John
10 MAY 1305 - Thomas Grelley had already by his charter, given to John de la Warre & Joan hi wife & the heirs of their bodies, the Manor of Portslade, Sussex & manor of Sixhills, Lincolnshire & 1310 they held Manors & Advowsons of Manchester & Curdly & Swineshead, with his wife Joan , of the Gift of Thomas Grelley her brother, to whom she was eventual heir
1351 - Joan, widow of John de la ware, & herself the heir of her brother Thomas Grelley, last Lord Manchester (d 1313) her son John died before his father, married Margaret s/o sir, Richard Holland at this date JOAN presented to church of Manchester - Thomas Rodefor

sourceList.title: Joan de Grelle La Warre, "Find A Grave Index"
The Barony of Grelley in The Victoria history of the county of Lancaster

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Grelley於大約1280年出生在英國藍卡爾時，她的父親Robert Grelley III28歲，母親Hawise de Burgh25歲。她於1294年11月19日之後與Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1353年享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Grelley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Irish   Crilly  .

variants.name: Crilly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Raghailligh ‘son of Raghailleach’. Compare Crawley .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Grelley was born about 1280, in Lancashire, England, her father, Robert Grelley III, was 28 and her mother, Hawise de Burgh, was 25. She married Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre after 19 November 1294, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1353, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Grelley est née environ 1280, à Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Grelley III, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Hawise de Burgh, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre après 19 novembre 1294, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1353, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Grelleyが約1280年にイギリスランカシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert Grelley IIIは28歳，母Hawise de Burghは25歳であった。1294年11月19日以後にイギリスイギリスでSir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1353年に74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Grelley(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Grelley III(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hawise de Burgh(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1294년 11월 19일 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1353년 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre após 19 de novembro de 1294, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1353, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Grelley родилась около 1280 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Grelley III было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise de Burgh, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre после 19 ноябрь 1294 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1353 г. в возрасте 74 лет.
